title: Application Containerization Initiative
summary: Containerized 20+ Java and Python applications with Docker, enabling consistent CI/CD across all environments.

content.value: Zycus applications were deployed directly to bare-metal and VM servers with environment-specific configuration baked in. 'Works on my machine' failures were common, and deployment scripts had to be maintained separately per environment.

Led the Docker containerization of 20+ applications across the Java and Python stack. Worked with each development team to produce multi-stage Dockerfiles that minimized image size and separated build-time from runtime dependencies.

Established a standardised base image library for Java (OpenJDK) and Python (3.x) with security patches applied. Images were pushed to AWS ECR via Jenkins pipelines and tagged with git SHA for immutable deployments. Docker Compose was adopted for local development parity.

Deployment failures due to environment inconsistencies dropped by 60%. Teams could reproduce any production issue locally using the exact container image. Foundation laid for later Kubernetes migration.
